picks.sp_analysis: Luinder Avila (KC): ERA 1.29, K/9 10.29, BB/9 3.86, WHIP 0.93 — elite ERA but elevated walk rate (3.86 BB/9) creates baserunner risk. No recent ERA data available. Chad Patrick (MIL): ERA 3.53, K/9 9.59, BB/9 3.02, WHIP 1.28 — SMALL SAMPLE (1 start), recent ERA 2.20 not reliable. Both SPs have control concerns (BB/9 3.0+) that support run-scoring opportunities.
picks.lineup_analysis: MIL OPS 0.959 — strong offensive lineup well above league average. KC OPS 0.852 — solid lineup. MIL's elite OPS is the primary lineup edge and supports OVER against a bullpen that will be taxed.
picks.bullpen_note: KC bullpen SEVERE — 3 fatigued arms (Steven Cruz, John Schreiber, Daniel Lynch IV). MIL bullpen FRESH — no fatigued arms. KC will need bullpen early given game context; SEVERE fatigue creates significant run-scoring risk in middle/late innings.
picks.umpire_note: Ryan Wills — rpg 9.1, slight OVER lean but below 9.2 threshold; K rate 22% neutral. Not counted as confirming factor.
picks.park_note: Kauffman Stadium: runs factor 0.97, HR factor 0.90 — slight pitcher lean. Does not support OVER independently.
picks.weather_impact: 27.4 mph SSW — blowing OUT to CF (OVER lean), 69F. Meaningful OVER lean — ball carries well at this wind speed and temperature.
picks.key_edge: KC bullpen SEVERE (3 arms: Cruz, Schreiber, Lynch IV) combined with MIL's elite OPS 0.959 and 27.4 mph wind blowing OUT at 69F creates meaningful run-scoring pressure in middle/late innings.
picks.rationale: KC's SEVERE bullpen fatigue (3 arms) is the primary driver — MIL's elite offense (OPS 0.959) will exploit fatigued relievers in the middle and late innings. Wind blowing OUT at 27.4 mph and 69F adds a meaningful OVER lean as the ball carries well to center field. While Avila's ERA 1.29 is elite, his BB/9 of 3.86 creates baserunner risk, and Patrick's ERA 3.53 gives KC's lineup (OPS 0.852) opportunities to score.
picks.flags: Rain 100% precip — postponement or scoring suppression risk is significant. Monitor game status. Avila ERA 1.29 based on 2025 data via ID lookup — small sample caveat applies.

picks.sp_analysis: Bryan Woo (SEA): ERA 2.94, K/9 9.57, BB/9 1.74, WHIP 0.93 — elite control and strong strikeout rate. SMALL SAMPLE (1 start), recent ERA 3.00 essentially matches season ERA. Reid Detmers (LAA): No season ERA data available (0 ERA listed); recent ERA 6.43 in 1 start (SMALL SAMPLE). The only available data for Detmers shows a 6.43 ERA, creating a gap of 3.49 runs favoring Woo. Woo's BB/9 1.74 is elite and limits free baserunners.
picks.lineup_analysis: SEA OPS 0.815 — solid lineup above 0.750 threshold, supports run-scoring. LAA OPS 0 — no data available; cannot confirm lineup strength. SEA's confirmed lineup quality combined with Woo's elite control creates a favorable run-differential environment.
picks.bullpen_note: LAA bullpen SEVERE — 2 fatigued arms (Ryan Zeferjahn, Brent Suter). SEA bullpen MODERATE — 1 fatigued arm (Cooper Criswell). LAA's SEVERE bullpen fatigue means run prevention will deteriorate in middle/late innings, supporting SEA covering -1.5.
picks.umpire_note: Ramon De Jesus — rpg 8.8, neutral. K rate 22% neutral. No meaningful lean in either direction.
picks.park_note: Angel Stadium: runs factor 0.96, HR factor 0.93 — slight pitcher lean. Marginally suppresses scoring but does not significantly impact run line analysis.
picks.weather_impact: 18.0 mph NE — crosswind, no directional scoring impact. 80F warm temperature. No weather edge in either direction.
picks.key_edge: SP ERA gap of 3.49 runs (Woo 2.94 vs Detmers 6.43 recent ERA) combined with LAA SEVERE bullpen fatigue (Zeferjahn, Suter) and plus-money run line odds (+105) creates strong positive EV on SEA -1.5.
picks.rationale: Bryan Woo's elite profile (ERA 2.94, BB/9 1.74, WHIP 0.93) faces a Detmers start with only a 6.43 recent ERA and no reliable season data — the SP gap strongly favors SEA. LAA's SEVERE bullpen (2 fatigued arms) means run prevention collapses after Detmers exits, giving SEA multiple opportunities to extend leads. The run line at +105 (plus money) provides exceptional value for a team with an 82.7% baseline win probability — SEA -1.5 at plus money is the highest-EV bet on this slate.

picks.bullpen_note: Braves MODERATE fatigue — 1 arm (Osvaldo Bido). Diamondbacks SEVERE fatigue — 3 arms (Andrew Hoffmann, Kevin Ginkel, Joe Ross). Arizona bullpen vulnerability is a meaningful edge for Atlanta in later innings.

picks.key_edge: Arizona bullpen SEVERE (3 fatigued arms) combined with massive Braves OPS advantage (1.194 vs 0.851, gap 0.343) at plus-money odds (-105).
picks.rationale: The Braves carry a dominant offensive edge with a 1.194 OPS versus Arizona's 0.851, a gap of 0.343 that is the largest single factor in this matchup. Arizona's bullpen is SEVERE with three fatigued arms (Hoffmann, Ginkel, Ross), meaning Atlanta's lineup will face a compromised relief corps in the middle and late innings. At -105 (near even money), the Braves offer positive EV given their offensive superiority and Arizona's bullpen vulnerability, even with both SPs in small-sample territory.
